macro_events.event_summary: Japan and China are leading a foreign government exodus from US Treasurys amid Gulf War fallout and dollar/currency concerns, with Chinese holdings hitting an 18-year low.
primary_drivers: Foreign demand decline at Treasury auctions pushes long-end yields higher
Dollar reserve status questioned → rotation into gold and hard assets
Higher term premium hurts long-duration assets and rate-sensitive sectors

beneficiaries.rationale: Newmont is the largest pure-play gold miner; foreign CB Treasury dumping historically coincides with reserve diversification into gold, lifting bullion prices and miner operating leverage.
Agnico Eagle is a senior gold producer with high earnings sensitivity to spot gold; benefits directly from reserve diversification bid.
Wheaton Precious Metals streaming model provides leveraged exposure to gold/silver price spike from reserve rotation without operating cost drag.
Intercontinental Exchange owns Treasury trading venues and clearing; higher Treasury volatility and volumes from foreign selling boost transaction revenue.
CME Group dominates Treasury futures and FX futures; surging hedging demand around UST volatility and dollar fears drives contract volumes.
MicroStrategy is the listed proxy for the bitcoin debasement-hedge thesis; de-dollarization narrative drives BTC bid which it is levered to.

losers.rationale: D.R. Horton is the largest US homebuilder with direct mortgage-rate demand sensitivity; rising 10y yields from foreign UST selling raises mortgage rates.
Charles Schwab carries large AFS/HTM Treasury portfolios; renewed long-end yield spike re-opens unrealized loss wound from 2023 regional bank stress.
Realty Income is a long-duration triple-net REIT explicitly traded as a bond proxy; rising 10y from foreign UST selling directly compresses the multiple.

headlines: Japan, China lead foreign government retreat from U.S. Treasurys as Gulf War fallout stokes currency fears

macro_events.event_summary: Home Depot reported Q1 2026 sales up 5% with management calling out a resilient core shopper despite higher gas prices.
primary_drivers: Resilient discretionary spend by home improvement / Pro customer
Peer read-through to home improvement and housing-adjacent retailers
Signal that higher gas prices are not yet crimping middle/upper-income consumer

beneficiaries.rationale: Lowe's is the closest pure-play peer to HD with ~75% overlapping product mix; a positive HD comp print historically drives same-day sympathy moves and analyst read-throughs on Pro/DIY demand.

Floor & Decor sells hard-surface flooring directly tied to home improvement / remodel demand; HD's resilient comp signals the remodel cycle isn't deteriorating, supporting FND's most-watched comp narrative.
Sherwin-Williams sells paint through HD and tracks remodel/repaint demand closely; a strong HD print supports the Pro contractor activity thesis that drives SHW's largest segment.
Tractor Supply shares the rural/Pro hardlines customer; HD's commentary on resilient core shopper supports the broader hardlines retail read despite gas headwinds.
Williams-Sonoma's home furnishings demand is correlated with home improvement / remodel activity; HD's beat undercuts the bear case that the home category is rolling over.

headlines: Home Depot says core shopper is resilient in the face of higher gas prices, sales rise 5%

macro_events.event_summary: Putin visits Xi in Beijing seeking deeper China support on energy purchases, technology/components, and diplomatic backing amid ongoing Ukraine war and Western sanctions.
primary_drivers: Deepening Russia-China axis raises Western secondary sanctions risk on Chinese firms
Continued redirection of Russian crude/gas to China entrenches discounted Asian energy flows
Geopolitical bloc-hardening reinforces defense spending and supply chain decoupling narratives

beneficiaries.rationale: Deepening Russia-China alignment reinforces the multi-year Western defense spending cycle; LMT is the most direct pure-play on US/NATO procurement (F-35, PAC-3, HIMARS) and benefits from sustained Ukraine-related munitions replenishment.
Patriot/Stinger/NASAMS demand directly tied to Ukraine resupply and NATO restocking; a visible Russia-China consolidation headline keeps the threat-environment thesis intact.
Strategic deterrence beneficiary (B-21, Sentinel ICBM, space) — narrative of two-front China+Russia challenge supports the strategic systems budget line.
Energy security and US-domiciled baseload generation gain relative appeal as the Russia-China energy bloc reduces fungibility of global gas/oil; Constellation is the cleanest US nuclear pure-play.

losers.rationale: Macau-concentrated revenue makes WYNN sensitive to a deteriorating US-China diplomatic backdrop and any tightening of cross-strait/sanctions rhetoric flowing from a Putin-Xi summit.

Continued Russian crude flowing to China at discount keeps marginal Asian barrels well-supplied, capping Brent upside; integrated majors lose the geopolitical risk premium tailwind on days when Russia-China energy cooperation is the headline.

headlines: Putin in Beijing: 3 things Russia needs from China

macro_events.event_summary: A MarketWatch opinion/analysis piece argues that structurally higher interest rates may be the new regime for the coming generation, warning of a regime shift away from the low-rate era.
primary_drivers: Higher discount rate compresses long-duration equity valuations
Higher net interest margins and reinvestment yields for financials/insurers
Rising debt service costs pressure leveraged balance sheets and rate-sensitive sectors (REITs, utilities, homebuilders)

beneficiaries.rationale: Schwab's earnings are highly levered to short rates via client cash sweep spreads and money market reinvestment; a structurally higher rate regime expands NIM and reduces the bull case for cash sorting reversal.

Life insurers like MetLife reinvest long-dated liability portfolios at higher yields; a sustained higher rate regime directly lifts spread income and reduces reserve strain on long-duration liabilities.
Higher rates and a structural rate regime shift drive sustained refinancing/issuance cycles and credit-rating demand; Moody's also earns float on cash. Note: opinion piece is low-impact catalyst.

losers.rationale: Realty Income is a bond-proxy net-lease REIT with ~6yr+ duration profile; higher structural rates raise its cost of capital and compress the spread investment model, plus dividend yield competes directly with risk-free rates.
D.R. Horton's volume is directly tied to mortgage affordability; a regime of structurally higher mortgage rates caps the addressable buyer pool and pressures margins via incentives/rate buydowns.
NextEra carries heavy debt to fund renewable capex; higher-for-longer rates raise refinancing costs and reduce the IRR spread on new project development versus risk-free benchmarks.

headlines: These charts offer a warning to the next generation — a new era of higher rates may be coming

macro_events.event_summary: Aluminum prices forecast to potentially reach $4,000/ton amid the most bullish supply/demand setup in over 50 years.
primary_drivers: Higher realized aluminum prices flow directly to primary producer margins
Smelter capacity constraints + power cost issues limit supply response
Downstream fabricators and aluminum-intensive buyers face input cost inflation

beneficiaries.rationale: Alcoa is the largest US-listed pure-play primary aluminum producer; ~every $100/ton move in LME aluminum translates roughly $150-200M to annual EBITDA. A move toward $4,000 from current ~$2,500 levels would be a step-change in earnings.
Century Aluminum is a near-pure-play US primary aluminum smelter with high operating leverage; smaller cap means greater percentage earnings sensitivity to LME price than AA.
Kaiser Aluminum is a fabricator but with pricing power tied to aluminum benchmarks via Midwest premium pass-through; benefits from rising contained-metal margins though less directly than smelters.
Aluminum Corp of China (Chalco) ADR — global primary aluminum producer with direct LME price leverage; trades on the same thesis as AA/CENX.

losers.rationale: Boeing is a major aluminum consumer for airframes; sustained $4,000/ton aluminum raises input costs on already-thin commercial aircraft margins, though long-term fixed-price contracts dampen near-term impact.
Anheuser-Busch InBev is one of the largest aluminum can buyers globally; rising aluminum costs (via Midwest premium) pressure packaging COGS for beverage makers.
Coca-Cola is heavily exposed to aluminum can costs; historical commentary shows aluminum is a top-3 commodity input. Sustained higher pricing pressures gross margin absent price hikes.
Ford's F-150 aluminum-body trucks are its highest-margin product; aluminum is a meaningful input cost and rising prices pressure auto gross margins more than peers using steel bodies.

headlines: Aluminum could hit $4,000 a ton amid most bullish set-up in over 50 years
